message:
fix(mobile): improve touch targets and fix visualization overflow

Address mobile usability issues discovered during viewport testing,
ensuring WCAG 2.1 compliant touch targets and proper content clipping.

apps/web/app/flywheel/page.tsx:

Touch target improvements (WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 2.5.5):
- Navigation "Back" button: size="default" with h-11 (44px height)
- "Get Started" button: size="default" with h-11 (44px height)
- Workflow card expand toggle: Added min-h-[44px] and py-3 padding
- These changes ensure minimum 44x44px touch targets on mobile

Typography refinements:
- Stats labels: Changed text-xs to text-[12px] for 4 stat items
  (Parallel agents, Projects, GitHub stars, Autonomous work)
- Explicit pixel value prevents browser minimum font-size overrides
- Maintains readability while fitting content on smaller screens

apps/web/components/flywheel-visualization.tsx:

Visualization overflow fix:
- Added responsive wrapper div with overflow-hidden
- Responsive max-widths: max-w-[312px] sm:max-w-[384px] md:max-w-[480px]
- Added aspect-square to maintain proportions during scaling
- Changed transform-origin from center to top-left
  (origin-top-left instead of implicit origin-center)
- Prevents horizontal scroll on mobile caused by scaled content
  extending beyond container bounds

Before: Flywheel visualization could cause horizontal scroll on
mobile devices when scaled content extended past viewport.

After: Content is properly clipped within responsive container,
maintaining visual appearance while preventing layout issues.

These fixes improve mobile experience per Playwright viewport tests
across iPhone SE (375px), iPhone 14 (390px), and Pixel 5 (393px).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
